description: Wikipedia article summary for Boston 1840s immigration

response.title: Boston
response.extract: Boston is the capital and most populous city of Massachusetts. In the 1840s, Boston was a major port of entry for immigrants, particularly Irish fleeing the Great Famine (1845-1852). The city's immigrant population surged — by 1850, over a quarter of Boston's residents were Irish-born. Passenger arrivals were recorded in customs passenger lists maintained at the Port of Boston. The Pilot, an Irish Catholic newspaper, published arrival notices and missing-persons advertisements. Immigrants settled in neighborhoods like the North End and Fort Hill.
